build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player builds modules on a grid surface by spending materials. Modules connect to adjacent ones. Three resource types (oxygen, food, power) are tracked with visible meters. Any resource reaching zero causes colonist deaths. Score 0 if there is no building mechanic, if resources are not tracked, or if depletion has no consequence. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Colonists are assigned to jobs (miner, farmer, engineer, researcher) and perform their role automatically. Job assignment is player-controlled. Idle colonists consume resources without producing. Score 0 if colonists have no jobs, if assignment is impossible, or if all colonists are identical. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Materials are mined from the asteroid by assigned miners. Materials are spent on building new modules. A material counter is visible and changes with mining and building. Score 0 if materials are infinite, if mining does not exist, or if building is free. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Meteor events strike randomly, damaging or destroying modules. The player must maintain redundancy or repair damaged modules. Damage is visible on affected modules. Score 0 if no threats exist or if modules are indestructible. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects: title -> new colony -> build/assign/manage -> time advances -> threats arrive -> colony thrives or dies -> result screen. The game ends when all colonists die or a population milestone is reached. Score 0 if any transition is missing or if there is no end condition. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least five module types exist (habitat, farm, solar panel, oxygen recycler, mine) with different sprites and functions. Each module contributes to a different resource or need. Score 0 if fewer than four types exist or if types are visually identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Colonists have morale affected by living conditions (habitat quality, workload, amenities). Low morale reduces productivity. A morale indicator is visible. Score 0 if morale does not exist or if it has no gameplay effect. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A research system unlocks advanced modules (greenhouse, fusion reactor, shield generator) by assigning researchers over time. At least three researchable upgrades exist. Score 0 if no research system exists or if all modules are available from the start. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Population grows when the colony has surplus resources and available habitat space. New colonists arrive or are born, increasing both workforce and consumption. Score 0 if population never changes or if growth is automatic without conditions. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The colony visibly transforms from a small lander to a sprawling base as modules are added. Early and late colony states look dramatically different. Score 0 if the colony appearance never changes regardless of building. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The HUD shows oxygen, food, power levels, population count, materials, and day counter in clear non-overlapping regions. Critical resource warnings are visible. Score 0 if resource meters or population is missing. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The colony view clearly shows the asteroid surface, placed modules with distinct appearances, colonist positions, and connection between modules. The build menu and play area are separated. Score 0 if modules are indistinguishable or if the grid is unreadable. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Actions produce visible feedback: building shows module appear, mining shows resources extracted, meteor strikes show impact and damage, resource depletion shows warning effects, and colonist death is shown. Score 0 if actions produce no observable change. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: asteroid surface, modules, colonists, UI, and effects belong to a consistent sci-fi frontier visual language with space-industrial tones. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Module sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art structures (domes, solar arrays, greenhouses) with distinct silhouettes per type. Colonists are visible character sprites. Score 0 if modules are plain colored shapes or if colonists are dots. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The asteroid surface uses real textured terrain (rocky, cratered) with a space backdrop (stars, distant planets). The environment feels like an alien world. Score 0 if the surface is a flat grey fill or if there is no space backdrop. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with sci-fi chrome: resource meters have holographic styling, build menu has tech-panel frames, and alerts have warning-light aesthetics. Score 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and atmosphere: colonists walk between modules, solar panels rotate, meteor strikes animate with impact effects, resource production shows visual activity, and the space backdrop has ambient motion (twinkling stars). Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
